description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring a striking pattern of alternating black and marbled gray stripes. The stripes run parallel to the shorter edge on two sides and perpendicular on the other two, creating a visually dynamic effect depending on the viewing angle. The block has a solid, monolithic appearance with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. The marbled gray stripes suggest a polished stone material, possibly granite or marble, while the black stripes appear matte and non-reflective. The sides of the block are solid black, contrasting with the patterned top. There are no visible engravings, markings, or signs of significant weathering on the surface.
description: A rectangular stone block with alternating black and marbled gray stripes on its flat top surface and solid black sides. The block has a polished, monolithic appearance with sharp edges.
description_view_0: The block is seen from the side with horizontal black and marbled gray stripes dominating the top surface.
description_view_1: The stripes on the top surface appear vertical, emphasizing the alternating pattern more distinctly.
description_view_2: Similar to view 0, the horizontal striped pattern is visible, but from the opposite side.
description_view_3: The vertical striped pattern is again prominent, viewed from the side opposite to view 1.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a decorative barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a leveled foundation
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surfaces appear clean and well-maintained with no chips or erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
